gpu: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (radeonsi, navi31, LLVM 19.1.1, DRM 3.59, 6.11.0-17-generic)
notes:
First initial minute is white screen where the intro video usually plays. After waiting about a minute, the menu is displayed and can be navigated without any issue.
Loading into a "line" is fast and snappy, but when it's done loading a pop-up shows "apiRenderMainWindow()"-error, and that the video cannot be found.
Train interior and HUD shows fine, but black video playback and can't drive the train.

It's currently impossible to play because the screen blacks out and the controls become erratic, making it unresponsive.
Using GeForce NOW on a Chromium-based browser seems to be the only viable way to play it right now.
Can see the menu after flickering and a white screen. Can select a line, but when pressing play, the game freezes.

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 480 (radeonsi, polaris10, ACO, DRM 3.64, 6.16.3+deb14-amd64)
notes:
Most game elements (settings, sound, cab, etc.) are functional now but movie files cannot be found on disk, leading to black screen and skipped launch intro. The game spits out 2 errors due to the inability to locate the files: "Movie file NOT FOUND" and "gOmvPlayer.IsOpen() = FALSE !!".

Also the game spits out a weird unknown error. 

Further down the line it'd be good if we can also get multi-monitor support. 

I think the fact it's not erroring on missing library means great progress was made, but more is to be done to put the game into a functional state. Good job so far, Valve.
If you dont change the route mid game everyting works as expected
Huge improvement, but chooses hardware and operating system
